Title: xCart — Get or Create Cart via Query
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER authenticated. cart query auto-creates user's default cart if none exists. NOTE: userId is listed as optional in schema but resolver returns Forbidden without it — always pass userId explicitly.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=get_cart] errors[] empty
[DATA label=get_cart] data.cart is non-null
[DATA label=get_cart] data.cart.id is non-empty GUID
[DATA label=get_cart] data.cart.items is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=get_cart] re-execute same query → same cart.id returned
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty with Forbidden code (userId missing); cart null; id empty or malformed

Title: xCart — addItem Adds Product to Cart
Preconditions: Runner-native. Uses @td(BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY.id) alias to guarantee a product with minQty=1 and no max cap — avoids PRODUCT_MIN_MAX_QTY silent rejection (root cause of REG-2026-04-29-0929 BUG-050b1-001). Clears cart, adds 1 item, verifies itemsCount + productId + quantity + validationErrors empty.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_one] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=add_one] data.addItem.validationErrors = 0
[COUNT label=add_one] data.addItem.itemsCount = 1
[DATA label=add_one] data.addItem.items.0.productId = @td(BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY.id)
[COUNT label=add_one] data.addItem.items.0.quantity = 1
[DATA label=add_one] data.addItem.items.0.id is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] items[0].listPrice.amount should be > 0 on a priced product — runner-manual
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; validationErrors non-empty (PRODUCT_MIN_MAX_QTY — product has min-qty rule, fix: update BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY alias); itemsCount not incremented; quantity mismatch; listPrice missing
References: xAPI; graphql-schema.md — Mutation.addItem; aliases.json BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY; REG-2026-04-29-0929

Title: xCart — changeCartItemQuantity Updates Qty
Preconditions: Runner-native. addItem qty=1, then changeCartItemQuantity to qty=5, verify quantity and subTotal recalc.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=change_qty] errors[] empty
[DATA label=change_qty] data.changeCartItemQuantity.items.0.id = {{LINE_ITEM_ID}}
[COUNT label=change_qty] data.changeCartItemQuantity.items.0.quantity = 5
[DATA label=change_qty] data.changeCartItemQuantity.subTotal is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] subTotal.amount = listPrice * 5 (runner-manual verification of totals recalc)
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; quantity still 1; subtotal not recalculated

Title: xCart — removeCartItem Removes Line Item
Preconditions: Runner-native. addItem then removeCartItem by lineItemId, verify itemsCount drops.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=remove_item] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=remove_item] data.removeCartItem.itemsCount = 0
[COUNT label=remove_item] data.removeCartItem.items.length = 0
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=remove_item] cart remains valid (id still present), just emptied
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; itemsCount unchanged; cart becomes null after removal
Cleanup: none — cart is empty after remove

Title: xCart — addOrUpdateCartShipment Sets Shipping Address
Preconditions: Runner-native. Requires cart with at least 1 item. Adds shipment with a New York deliveryAddress, then re-queries cart to verify address persisted.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=set_shipment] errors[] empty
[DATA label=set_shipment] data.addOrUpdateCartShipment.id is non-null
[DATA label=set_shipment] data.addOrUpdateCartShipment.shipments.0.deliveryAddress.city = New York
[DATA label=set_shipment] data.addOrUpdateCartShipment.shipments.0.deliveryAddress.countryCode = USA
[DATA label=set_shipment] data.addOrUpdateCartShipment.shipments.0.deliveryAddress.postalCode = 10001
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=set_shipment] cart query confirms shipment.deliveryAddress fields populated
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; address fields null; validationErrors on shipment; countryCode or regionId wrong

Title: xCart — addCoupon Applies Valid and Rejects Invalid
Preconditions: Runner-native. Applies a seeded valid coupon (QA10OFF via @td alias) then an invalid one to verify graceful rejection.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=apply_valid] errors[] empty
[DATA label=apply_valid] data.addCoupon.coupons is non-null
[COUNT label=apply_valid] data.addCoupon.coupons.length >= 1
[DATA label=apply_valid] data.addCoupon.discountTotal.amount > 0
[ERRORS label=apply_invalid] errors[] empty
[DATA label=apply_invalid] data.addCoupon.coupons is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] apply_valid: at least one entry in coupons[] has isAppliedSuccessfully=true (verify exact code matches alias); discountTotal.amount > 0 means coupon produced a real discount.
[EVIDENCE] apply_invalid: xAPI contract — invalid codes ARE returned in coupons[] with isAppliedSuccessfully=false rather than via top-level errors[] (see CRI-GQL-091 pattern). Silent no-op via empty errors[] is acceptable here; assertion verifies the response shape is structured.
Failure_Signals: HTTP 500 on coupon apply; stack trace in errors[].message; valid coupon rejected (discountTotal=0); invalid coupon silently accepted as applied (would inflate discountTotal)

Title: xCart — Full Checkout Flow via GraphQL
Preconditions: Runner-native but CREATES A REAL ORDER — no easy teardown via runner. Sequence: addItem → addOrUpdateCartShipment (FixedRate w/ price) → addOrUpdateCartPayment (DefaultManualPaymentMethod) → createOrderFromCart. Uses @td(BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY.id) to guarantee a product with minQty=1 so addItem succeeds — prevents downstream createOrderFromCart ValidationError (REG-2026-04-29-0929 BUG-050b1-002). See order-creation-matrix.md § GraphQL xAPI Checkout Flow for required price-match on shipment.
Assertions:
[COUNT label=add_item] data.addItem.validationErrors = 0
[ERRORS label=set_shipment] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=set_payment] errors[] empty
[ERRORS label=create_order] errors[] empty
[DATA label=create_order] data.createOrderFromCart.id is non-empty GUID
[DATA label=create_order] data.createOrderFromCart.number is non-null
[DATA label=create_order] data.createOrderFromCart.status is non-null
Failure_Signals: add_item validationErrors non-empty (PRODUCT_MIN_MAX_QTY — product has min-qty rule, fix BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY alias); any mutation errors[] non-empty; cart totals stale; availableShippingMethods empty; order not created
Cleanup: Test creates a real order — cancel via Admin API if needed (no runner-side teardown)
References: xAPI; graphql-schema.md; order-creation-matrix.md § GraphQL xAPI Checkout Flow; aliases.json BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY; REG-2026-04-29-0929

Title: xCart — updateCartQuantity Single Item Happy Path
Preconditions: Runner-native. Uses @td(BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY.id) to guarantee addItem qty=1 succeeds (no PRODUCT_MIN_MAX_QTY), then updateCartQuantity sets qty=3 via items[{productId, quantity}]. items[] list must use productId, not lineItemId.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_one] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=add_one] data.addItem.validationErrors = 0
[ERRORS label=bulk_update] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=bulk_update] data.updateCartQuantity.itemsCount = 1
[DATA label=bulk_update] data.updateCartQuantity.items.0.productId = @td(BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY.id)
[COUNT label=bulk_update] data.updateCartQuantity.items.0.quantity = 3
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=bulk_update] cart query confirms qty=3 persisted
Failure_Signals: add_one validationErrors non-empty (PRODUCT_MIN_MAX_QTY — update BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY alias); errors[] non-empty; quantity still 1; items[] duplicated

Title: xCart — updateCartQuantity Bulk Updates Multiple Items
Preconditions: Runner-native. Uses @td(BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY.id) as PRODUCT_1. PRODUCT_2 is resolved via inline products query (first:2, skip items[0] which is PRODUCT_1). addItem each with qty=1, then bulk-update via updateCartQuantity to qty=2/4. NOTE: Both addItem calls include validationErrors projection — guards against silent PRODUCT_MIN_MAX_QTY rejection.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_first] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=add_first] data.addItem.validationErrors = 0
[ERRORS label=add_second] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=add_second] data.addItem.validationErrors = 0
[ERRORS label=bulk_update] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=bulk_update] data.updateCartQuantity.itemsCount = 2
[COUNT label=bulk_update] data.updateCartQuantity.items.length = 2
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] Verify both items in response have the updated quantities (2 + 4) matching inputs — runner-manual evidence check
[EVIDENCE] If add_second validationErrors non-empty, PRODUCT_2 has a min-qty rule — try a different second product from the catalog probe
Failure_Signals: add_first/add_second validationErrors non-empty (PRODUCT_MIN_MAX_QTY — update alias or pick different second product); errors[] non-empty; itemsCount = 1 (partial); both items quantity = 1 (update ignored)

Title: xCart — updateCartQuantity quantity=0 Removes Item
Preconditions: Runner-native. Uses @td(BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY.id) to guarantee addItem qty=1 succeeds. Verifies setting quantity=0 via updateCartQuantity removes the item (expected per legacy spec).
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_one] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=add_one] data.addItem.validationErrors = 0
[ERRORS label=set_zero] errors[] empty
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] set_zero: either data.updateCartQuantity.itemsCount=0 with items[] empty (qty=0 removes) OR items[].quantity=0 retained (qty=0 kept — surprising). Document observed branch.
Failure_Signals: add_one validationErrors non-empty (PRODUCT_MIN_MAX_QTY — update BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY alias); HTTP 500; item remains with qty=1; stack trace in errors[].message

Preconditions: BLOCKED: Requires LOW_STOCK_SKU fixture (aliases.json PROD_LOW_STOCK — template only, NOT seeded in QA). Once seeded, test addItem qty=999 against 5-unit stock → expect errors[] non-empty with stock-exceeded message OR clamp to stock-level. NOTE: setup_prod step removed — once unblocked, use @td(PROD_LOW_STOCK.id) for the product ID instead of an open-ended products query.
Steps: [MANUAL-BLOCKED] Needs LOW_STOCK_SKU env var + seeded product with stock=5. See aliases.json PROD_LOW_STOCK. Execute once seeded. Use @td(PROD_LOW_STOCK.id) as PRODUCT_ID — do NOT use open-ended setup_prod filter as it may return products with min_qty rules.
Failure_Signals: quantity=999 accepted silently (oversold risk); no validation message returned

Title: xCart — updateCartQuantity Non-Existent ProductId
Preconditions: Runner-native. Attempts updateCartQuantity on a zero-GUID productId. Verifies structured error or graceful no-op.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=update_bogus] errors[] empty
[DATA label=update_bogus] data.updateCartQuantity.id is non-null
[COUNT label=update_bogus] data.updateCartQuantity.itemsCount == 0
[COUNT label=update_bogus] data.updateCartQuantity.items.length == 0
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] Silent no-op contract: errors[] empty, cart remains empty (itemsCount=0, items[] empty). The zero-GUID productId is not resolved → no phantom line item. NO HTTP 500 or stack trace.
Failure_Signals: HTTP 500; phantom line item created for non-existent product (P0); existing items corrupted; stack trace in errors[]; itemsCount > 0 (would mean cart was modified)

Title: xCart — updateCartQuantity Totals Recalculated After Bulk Update
Preconditions: Runner-native. Uses @td(BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY.id) as PRODUCT_1. PRODUCT_2 resolved via inline products query (items[1] — second item in catalog page, same filter as CRB-GQL-047). Add 2 items qty=1 each, bulk-update qty=2/3, verify subTotal recalculated.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_first] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=add_first] data.addItem.validationErrors = 0
[ERRORS label=add_second] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=add_second] data.addItem.validationErrors = 0
[ERRORS label=bulk_update] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=bulk_update] data.updateCartQuantity.itemsCount = 2
[DATA label=bulk_update] data.updateCartQuantity.subTotal is non-null
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[EVIDENCE] Verify bulk_update.subTotal.amount > add_second.subTotal.amount (qty increased → total increased). extendedPrice[i].amount = listPrice × quantity.
[EVIDENCE] If add_second validationErrors non-empty, PRODUCT_2 (items[1]) has a min-qty rule — catalog ordering may have changed; probe for another buyable-no-min-qty product.
Failure_Signals: add_first/add_second validationErrors non-empty (PRODUCT_MIN_MAX_QTY); errors[] non-empty; subTotal unchanged after qty bump; subTotal decreased; extendedPrice mismatch (stale cache)
References: xAPI; graphql-schema.md — Mutation.updateCartQuantity MoneyType; aliases.json BUYABLE_NO_MIN_QTY

Title: xCart — clearCart Empties Cart With Items
Preconditions: Runner-native. Adds 2 items, calls clearCart, verifies cart is emptied but still valid.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=clear_it] errors[] empty
[DATA label=clear_it] data.clearCart.id is non-null
[COUNT label=clear_it] data.clearCart.itemsCount = 0
[COUNT label=clear_it] data.clearCart.items.length = 0
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] clearCart.subTotal.amount should be 0 after clear — runner-manual
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; itemsCount > 0 after clear; items[] non-empty; cart.id null (should not be deleted)

Title: xCart — clearCart on Already-Empty Cart is Idempotent
Preconditions: Runner-native. Calls clearCart twice consecutively — both must succeed (idempotent, no HTTP 500).
Steps:
[AUTH role=ORG_USER]
[GQL-OP setup_me]
  query { me { id } }
[GQL-EXEC setup_me]
[GQL-CAPTURE setup_me.data.me.id → USER_ID]
[GQL-OP clear_first]
  mutation { clearCart(command: { storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" userId: "{{USER_ID}}" }) { id itemsCount } }
[GQL-EXEC clear_first]
[GQL-OP clear_second]
  mutation { clearCart(command: { storeId: "{{STORE_ID}}" userId: "{{USER_ID}}" }) { id itemsCount } }
[GQL-EXEC clear_second]
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=clear_first] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=clear_first] data.clearCart.itemsCount = 0
[ERRORS label=clear_second] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=clear_second] data.clearCart.itemsCount = 0
Cross_Layer_Checks: [ROUNDTRIP label=clear_second] same cart.id returned across both clears (idempotent)
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty on second clear; HTTP 500; unhandled exception

Title: xCart — addBulkItemsCart Coalesces Duplicate SKUs Into Single Line Item
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER. Submits the same SKU 1507112554 three times (quantity 2 each) in one addBulkItemsCart call to verify BL-CART-007: same-SKU repeats MUST coalesce into ONE cart line with summed quantity (6), not create 3 duplicate lines. SKU 1507112554 (Coca Cola Regular 6x330ml, SKU 1507112554) verified buyable on B2B-store in QA 2026-04-30. Note: addBulkItemsCart input uses productSku (not productId — differs from addItem/addItemsCart) and returns BulkCartType { cart, errors }.
Test_Data: SKU=1507112554 (Coca Cola Regular 6x330ml; verified live in QA 2026-04-30). Documented duplicate-SKU bulk-add example.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=bulk_add_dup] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=bulk_add_dup] data.addBulkItemsCart.errors = 0
[COUNT label=bulk_add_dup] data.addBulkItemsCart.cart.itemsCount = 1
[COUNT label=bulk_add_dup] data.addBulkItemsCart.cart.itemsQuantity = 6
[DATA label=bulk_add_dup] data.addBulkItemsCart.cart.items.0.sku = 1507112554
[COUNT label=bulk_add_dup] data.addBulkItemsCart.cart.items.0.quantity = 6
[DATA label=bulk_add_dup] data.addBulkItemsCart.cart.items.0.id is non-null
[ERRORS label=verify_cart] errors[] empty
[COUNT label=verify_cart] data.cart.itemsCount = 1
[COUNT label=verify_cart] data.cart.itemsQuantity = 6
[COUNT label=verify_cart] data.cart.items.0.quantity = 6
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] verify_cart query right after bulk_add_dup must show ONE row for sku 1507112554 with quantity 6 — never 3 separate rows of quantity 2 (BL-CART-007 line-item dedupe invariant)
Failure_Signals: errors[] non-empty; data.addBulkItemsCart.errors[] non-empty (e.g. PRODUCT_MIN_MAX_QTY — pick a different verified SKU); cart.items contains 3 separate rows for sku 1507112554 (BL-CART-007 violation); itemsCount=3 instead of 1; itemsQuantity ≠ 6; productSku rejected because mutation was called with productId (schema requires productSku for InputNewBulkItemType)
Cleanup: none — clearCart at end of steps
References: xAPI; graphql-schema.md — Mutation.addBulkItemsCart / InputAddBulkItemsType / InputNewBulkItemType; test-data/graphql/mutations/addBulkItemsCart.graphql; BL-CART-007

Title: [VCST-4936] addItem ×2 for Same SKU Accumulates Quantity to 2 (BL-CART-007)
Preconditions: Runner-native. ORG_USER. VCST-4936 BL-CART-007 verification via standard addItem mutation (not the deprecated addBulkItemsCart). Two consecutive addItem calls with same productId, qty=1 each → one lineItem with quantity=2. Pre-fix Quick Order symptom: duplicate-SKU lines in bulk-add lost second-entry quantity. This case proves the cart's quantity-accumulation invariant works at the xCart layer.
Assertions:
[ERRORS label=add_1] errors[] empty
[DATA label=add_1] data.addItem.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=add_2] errors[] empty
[DATA label=add_2] data.addItem.itemsCount = 1
[ERRORS label=read_back] errors[] empty
[DATA label=read_back] data.cart.itemsCount = 1
[DATA label=read_back] data.cart.itemsQuantity = 5
Cross_Layer_Checks: [EVIDENCE] BL-CART-007: same-SKU duplicate add accumulates qty (2+3=5) on a single lineItem; itemsCount stays 1.
References: xAPI; Mutation.addItem; VCST-4936 (Quick Order regression); BL-CART-007 same-SKU merge rule
